chicken stir-fry with rice.name: Chicken Stir-fry with Rice
ingredients: 1 lb boneless, skinless chicken breast or thighs, cut into 1-inch pieces
2 tbsp soy sauce (or tamari for gluten-free), divided
1 tbsp vegetable oil (or other high-heat cooking oil)
1 head broccoli, cut into small florets
1 cup snap peas or snow peas
2 carrots, peeled and thinly sliced or julienned
Cooked jasmine or long-grain rice, for serving (approx. 3 cups cooked)
instructions: **Prepare Chicken:** In a medium bowl, combine the chicken pieces with 1 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tsp rice vinegar, 1/2 tsp sesame oil, and 1 tsp cornstarch. Toss to coat and set aside to marinate for at least 10 minutes (or up to 30 minutes).
**Prepare Stir-fry Sauce:** In a small bowl, whisk together the remaining 1 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tbsp rice vinegar, 1/2 tsp sesame oil, 1 tsp cornstarch, chicken broth, and brown sugar/honey. Set aside.
**Cook Rice:** Prepare rice according to package directions. Keep warm.

**Stir-fry Chicken:** Heat 1 tbsp vegetable oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken and stir-fry for 3-5 minutes, until cooked through and lightly browned. Remove chicken from the pan and set aside.
**Stir-fry Vegetables:** If needed, add a little more oil to the pan. Add sliced onion, minced garlic, and grated ginger. Stir-fry for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add broccoli florets and carrots to the pan. Stir-fry for 3-4 minutes until they start to become tender-crisp.
Add red bell pepper and snap peas. Continue to stir-fry for another 2-3 minutes.

**Combine and Sauce:** Return the cooked chicken to the pan with the vegetables. Give the prepared stir-fry sauce a quick whisk (cornstarch settles at the bottom) and pour it evenly over the chicken and vegetables.
Stir continuously for 1-2 minutes as the sauce thickens and coats all the ingredients.
**Serve:** Remove from heat and serve immediately over hot cooked rice.

oatmeal with berries.name: Oatmeal with Berries
ingredients: 1 cup water or milk (dairy or non-dairy)
1/2 cup fresh or frozen berries (strawberries, blueberries, raspberries, etc.)
1 tablespoon sweetener (honey, maple syrup, sugar, or artificial sweetener, optional)
1 tablespoon nuts or seeds (optional, for topping)
instructions: Combine oats, water or milk, and salt (if using) in a saucepan.
Bring to a boil over medium-high heat, then reduce heat to low and simmer, stirring occasionally, for 5-7 minutes, or until oats are tender and the liquid is mostly absorbed.
If using frozen berries, you can add them during the last 2-3 minutes of cooking to warm them through. If using fresh berries, you can stir them in after cooking or use them as a topping.
Remove from heat and let stand for a minute or two.
Pour oatmeal into a bowl.
Top with berries, sweetener (if desired), nuts or seeds (if using), and a pinch of cinnamon (if desired).
Serve immediately.

tuna salad sandwich.name: Tuna Salad Sandwich
ingredients: Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
instructions: In a medium bowl, flake the drained tuna with a fork.
Add mayonnaise, chopped celery, chopped onion, and sweet pickle relish (if using).
Stir in lemon juice (if using).
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Mix well until all ingredients are combined.
Spread the tuna salad mixture evenly on two slices of bread.
Top with lettuce leaves, if desired.
Cover with the remaining two slices of bread.
Cut sandwiches in half, if desired, and serve.

pasta with tomato sauce and meatballs.name: Pasta with Tomato Sauce and Meatballs
instructions: In a large bowl, combine ground beef, breadcrumbs, Parmesan cheese, beaten egg, minced garlic, chopped parsley, salt, and pepper. Mix well and form into 1-inch meatballs.
Heat olive oil in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Add meatballs and brown on all sides. Remove meatballs and set aside.
Add chopped onion to the pot and cook until softened, about 5-7 minutes.
Stir in crushed tomatoes, dried basil, dried oregano, and red pepper flakes (if using). Bring to a simmer.
Return meatballs to the pot. Cover and simmer for at least 30 minutes, or until meatballs are cooked through and sauce has thickened.
While the sauce is simmering, cook pasta according to package directions. Drain.
Serve meatballs and sauce over cooked pasta. Garnish with grated Parmesan cheese.

chicken salad sandwich.name: Chicken Salad Sandwich
ingredients: 2 cups cooked chicken, shredded or diced
1/8 teaspoon black pepper, or to taste
8 slices of bread (e.g., white, whole wheat, or sourdough)
instructions: In a medium bowl, combine the cooked chicken, mayonnaise, celery, and red onion.
Add the lemon juice, salt, and pepper. Stir until all ingredients are well combined.
Taste the mixture and adjust the seasoning if necessary.
To assemble the sandwiches, place a lettuce leaf on four of the bread slices.
Spoon a generous amount of the chicken salad mixture onto the lettuce.
Top with a slice of tomato, if using.
Place the remaining four slices of bread on top to complete the sandwiches.
Serve immediately or chill for later.

roast chicken with roasted vegetables.name: Roast Chicken with Roasted Vegetables
ingredients: 1 teaspoon black pepper, or to taste
1 head of garlic, cut in half crosswise
2 lbs (1 kg) mixed root vegetables (e.g., potatoes, carrots, onions), peeled and chopped into 1-inch pieces
instructions: Preheat your oven to 425°F (220°C).
Pat the chicken dry with paper towels. Rub 1 tablespoon of olive oil all over the outside of the chicken. Season generously inside and out with salt and pepper.
Stuff the chicken cavity with the halved lemon, halved garlic head, and half of the rosemary and thyme sprigs.
In a large roasting pan, toss the chopped vegetables with the remaining 1 tablespoon of olive oil, the rest of the herbs, and a sprinkle of salt and pepper. Arrange them in a single layer.
Place the seasoned chicken on top of the vegetables in the roasting pan. Truss the legs with kitchen twine if desired.

Roast in the preheated oven for about 1 hour and 15-30 minutes. The chicken is cooked when the juices run clear when a thigh is pierced and a meat thermometer inserted into the thickest part of the thigh reads 165°F (74°C).
Remove the chicken and vegetables from the oven. Transfer the chicken to a cutting board and let it rest for 10-15 minutes before carving. This allows the juices to redistribute.
Serve the carved chicken immediately with the roasted vegetables from the pan.

scrambled eggs on toast.name: Scrambled Eggs on Toast
ingredients: 1/4 cup whole milk or heavy cream
Freshly ground black pepper to taste
instructions: In a medium bowl, whisk together the eggs, milk, salt, and pepper until the mixture is smooth and pale yellow.
Toast the bread slices to your desired level of brownness.
Melt the butter in a non-stick skillet or saucepan over medium-low heat. Ensure the butter is melted but not browning.
Pour the egg mixture into the skillet. Let it cook undisturbed for about 20-30 seconds, until the edges just begin to set.
Using a rubber spatula, gently push the eggs from the edges toward the center of the pan, creating large, soft curds.
Continue to gently stir and fold the eggs every few seconds until they are cooked through but still moist and glossy. This should take about 2-3 minutes.
Remove the skillet from the heat immediately to prevent overcooking.
Spoon the hot scrambled eggs onto the toasted bread slices.
Garnish with fresh chives if desired and serve immediately.

leftover roast chicken and vegetables.name: Leftover Roast Chicken and Vegetables
ingredients: 2 cups leftover roast chicken, shredded or diced
3 cups leftover roasted vegetables (e.g., potatoes, carrots, broccoli, parsnips), roughly chopped
1/4 cup chicken stock or water (optional, to moisten)
Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
1 tablespoon fresh parsley, chopped (for garnish, optional)
instructions: Heat the olive oil or butter in a large skillet or frying pan over medium-high heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 3-4 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.
Add the leftover chicken and vegetables to the skillet. Stir to combine.
Cook for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until everything is heated through. If using potatoes, allow them to sit for a few minutes without stirring to become crispy.
If the mixture seems dry, pour in the chicken stock or water and stir to combine.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh parsley if desired.

leftover chicken stir-fry.name: Leftover Chicken Stir-fry
ingredients: 2 cups cooked chicken, shredded or cubed
1 tablespoon vegetable oil or sesame oil
4 cups mixed vegetables (e.g., broccoli florets, sliced bell peppers, carrots, snap peas)
Cooked rice or noodles, for serving
Optional: Green onions and sesame seeds for garnish
instructions: Prepare the sauce: In a small bowl, whisk together the soy sauce, rice vinegar, and honey/brown sugar. In a separate small bowl, mix the cornstarch with the cold water to create a slurry. Set both aside.
Heat the oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat.
Add the onion and stir-fry for 2 minutes until it begins to soften. Add the garlic and ginger and cook for 30 seconds more until fragrant.
Add the harder vegetables like carrots and broccoli to the skillet. Stir-fry for 3-4 minutes.
Add the softer vegetables like bell peppers and snap peas. Continue to stir-fry for another 2-3 minutes until all vegetables are crisp-tender.
Add the cooked chicken to the skillet and toss to combine. Cook for 1-2 minutes until the chicken is heated through.
Pour the soy sauce mixture over the ingredients and stir to coat everything evenly. Bring to a simmer.
Give the cornstarch slurry a quick stir, then pour it into the skillet. Cook, stirring constantly, until the sauce thickens, about 1-2 minutes.
Remove from heat and serve immediately over a bed of rice or noodles. Garnish with sliced green onions and sesame seeds, if desired.

chicken and tomato pasta.name: Chicken and Tomato Pasta
ingredients: 1 pound penne or your favorite pasta
1 pound boneless, skinless chicken breast, cut into 1-inch cubes
1/2 cup chicken broth or white wine
Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
1/4 cup grated Parmesan cheese, for serving
instructions: Cook pasta according to package directions until al dente. Drain, reserving about 1/2 cup of the pasta water, and set aside.
While the pasta is cooking, season the chicken cubes generously with salt and pepper. Heat the olive oil in a large skillet or Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the chicken to the skillet in a single layer and cook until golden brown and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes. Remove the chicken from the skillet and set aside on a plate.
In the same skillet, reduce the heat to medium. Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.
Pour in the crushed tomatoes, chicken broth, dried oregano, and dried basil. Stir to combine, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pan.
Bring the sauce to a simmer and let it cook for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it has thickened slightly.
Return the cooked chicken to the skillet. Stir to combine and heat through.

Add the cooked pasta to the skillet with the sauce. Toss everything together until the pasta is well-coated. If the sauce is too thick, add a splash of the reserved pasta water to reach your desired consistency.
Season with additional salt and pepper to taste. Serve immediately, garnished with grated Parmesan cheese and fresh basil leaves.

leftover chicken pasta.name: Leftover Chicken Pasta
ingredients: 2 cups cooked leftover chicken, shredded or chopped
1/2 cup grated Parmesan cheese, plus more for serving
Salt and black pepper to taste
Fresh parsley or basil for garnish (optional)
instructions: Cook pasta according to package directions. Drain, reserving about 1/2 cup of the pasta water.
While pasta is cooking, heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.
Stir in the diced tomatoes with their juice and the Italian seasoning. Bring to a simmer and cook for 5-7 minutes, breaking up the tomatoes with a spoon, until the sauce thickens slightly.
Reduce heat to low. Stir in the heavy cream and Parmesan cheese until the cheese is melted and the sauce is smooth.
Add the leftover chicken to the sauce and stir to combine. Heat through for 2-3 minutes.
Add the drained pasta to the skillet with the sauce. Toss to coat everything evenly. If the sauce is too thick, add a splash of the reserved pasta water to reach your desired consistency.
Season with salt and pepper to taste. Serve immediately, garnished with extra Parmesan cheese and fresh parsley or basil, if desired.

chicken curry with rice.name: Chicken Curry with Rice
ingredients: 1 lb boneless, skinless chicken breast, cut into 1-inch cubes
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Cook the rice according to package directions. Typically, combine 1 cup of rice with 2 cups of water or broth, bring to a boil, then reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 15-20 minutes until liquid is absorbed.
While the rice cooks, heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet or pot over medium-high heat.
Add the chopped onion and sauté until softened, about 5 minutes. Add the garlic and ginger and cook for another minute until fragrant.
Stir in the curry powder, turmeric, and cumin. Cook for 30 seconds, stirring constantly, until spices are toasted and fragrant.
Add the chicken pieces to the skillet. Season with salt and pepper and cook until lightly browned on all sides.
Pour in the diced tomatoes (with their juice) and the coconut milk. Stir to combine, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pan.
Bring the mixture to a simmer, then reduce the heat to low. Cover and cook for 15-20 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and the sauce has thickened slightly.
Taste and adjust seasoning with more salt and pepper if needed.
Serve the chicken curry hot over the cooked rice, garnished with fresh cilantro.

leftover chicken curry.name: Leftover Chicken Curry
ingredients: 2-3 cups cooked leftover chicken, shredded or cubed
1 cup frozen peas or chopped spinach (optional)
Cooked rice or naan, for serving
instructions: Heat the oil or ghee in a large skillet or pot over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5-7 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic and grated ginger, and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the curry powder, turmeric, and cumin. Stir constantly for 30 seconds until the spices are toasted and aromatic.
Pour in the diced tomatoes with their juices and the coconut milk. Stir to combine, scraping up any bits from the bottom of the pan.
Bring the mixture to a simmer, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and let it cook for 10-15 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Stir in the leftover chicken and the optional peas or spinach. Add the broth if the sauce is too thick.
Continue to simmer for another 5-10 minutes, or until the chicken is heated through completely.
Season with salt and pepper to your liking.
Serve hot over a bed of rice or with warm naan bread, garnished with fresh cilantro.

chicken noodle soup.name: Chicken Noodle Soup
ingredients: 1 pound boneless, skinless chicken breasts or thighs
Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the chopped onion, carrots, and celery. Cook, stirring occasionally, until softened, about 5-7 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.
Pour in the chicken broth. Add the whole chicken breasts, bay leaf, and dried thyme. Bring the soup to a boil, then reduce the heat to a simmer.
Cover and cook for 15-20 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and no longer pink inside.
Carefully remove the chicken from the pot and place it on a cutting board. Let it cool slightly, then shred the meat using two forks.
While the chicken is cooling, bring the broth back to a gentle boil. Add the uncooked egg noodles and cook according to package directions, usually 6-8 minutes, until tender.
Return the shredded chicken to the pot. Stir in the fresh parsley.
Season generously with salt and black pepper to your taste.
Remove the bay leaf before serving. Ladle into bowls and serve hot.

leftover chicken noodle soup.name: Leftover Chicken Noodle Soup
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
3 cups cooked leftover chicken, shredded or diced
instructions: Heat olive oil or butter in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add onion, carrots, and celery. Sauté until softened, about 5-7 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.
Pour in the chicken broth, add thyme, the bay leaf, salt, and pepper. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to a simmer.
Cover and simmer for 15-20 minutes to let the vegetables become tender and the flavors meld.
Increase the heat to bring the soup back to a boil. Add the egg noodles and cook according to package directions, typically 7-9 minutes, until al dente.
Stir in the leftover chicken and cook until just heated through, about 2-3 minutes.
Remove the pot from the heat, discard the bay leaf, and stir in the fresh parsley.
Taste and adjust seasoning with more salt and pepper if desired before serving.

chicken burgers.name: Chicken Burgers
ingredients: Optional toppings: lettuce, tomato, cheese, mayonnaise
instructions: In a large bowl, combine the ground chicken, breadcrumbs, egg, onion, garlic, parsley, salt, and pepper.
Mix gently with your hands until just combined. Do not overmix.
Divide the mixture into 4 equal portions and shape into patties about 1/2-inch thick.
Heat the olive oil in a large skillet or on a grill over medium-high heat.
Cook the patties for 5-7 minutes per side, or until golden brown and cooked through (the internal temperature reaches 165°F or 74°C).
If using cheese, place a slice on top of each patty during the last minute of cooking to melt.
Lightly toast the burger buns, if desired.
Assemble the burgers by placing the cooked patties on the buns and adding your favorite toppings. Serve immediately.

chicken salad.name: Classic Chicken Salad
ingredients: 3 cups cooked and shredded or diced chicken
1/4 cup chopped fresh dill or parsley (optional)
Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
instructions: In a medium bowl, combine the shredded chicken, mayonnaise, celery, and red onion.
Add the lemon juice and optional fresh herbs. Stir gently until everything is well combined.
Season with salt and pepper to your preference. Taste and adjust seasonings if necessary.
For best results, cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Serve chilled on sandwiches, with crackers, or over a bed of lettuce.

pan-fried chicken thighs with rice.name: Pan-fried Chicken Thighs with Rice
instructions: Rinse 1 cup of rice under cold water. In a medium saucepan, combine the rice and 2 cups of water or broth. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until the liquid is absorbed. Remove from heat, let stand covered for 5-10 minutes, then fluff with a fork.
While the rice cooks, pat the chicken thighs thoroughly dry with paper towels.
In a small bowl, mix together the salt, black pepper, garlic powder, and paprika. Rub the spice mixture evenly over all sides of the chicken thighs.
Heat the olive oil in a large skillet (cast iron is recommended) over medium-high heat until it shimmers.
Carefully place the chicken thighs skin-side down in the hot pan. Cook undisturbed for 10-12 minutes, until the skin is deep golden brown and crispy.

Flip the chicken thighs. Reduce the heat to medium-low, cover the skillet, and continue to cook for another 10-15 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and the internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C) with a meat thermometer.
Remove the chicken from the pan and let it rest on a cutting board for 5 minutes before serving.
Serve the pan-fried chicken thighs hot with the fluffy rice. Garnish with chopped parsley if desired.
